title: if_cli_opencode YAML branches do not pass --pure, breaking cli-opencode dispatch under DeepSeek-family models
scopeProof: Grep across 4 deep-* YAML files: opencode run present, --pure absent in all 4 if_cli_opencode branches
evidence: All 4 if_cli_opencode branches dispatch opencode run with --dangerously-skip-permissions but no --pure. Pre-flight smoke recorded at deep-review-state.jsonl:2 confirms DeepSeek API rejects MCP tool names with regex pattern violation under default plugins; --pure works; YAML does not pass it.
recommendation: Add --pure to all 4 opencode run invocations after --dangerously-skip-permissions and before {optional_variant_flag}; document DeepSeek-family --pure requirement in cli-opencode SKILL.md.
downgradeTrigger: If a maintainer documents cli-opencode as intentionally not designed for DeepSeek-family models AND adds a guard in parseExecutorConfig that rejects DeepSeek model strings for cli-opencode, downgrade to P2.

title: cli-opencode advisor alias claim in 101 strategy not reflected in aliases.ts
scopeProof: Read aliases.ts:1-49 fully; RAW_ALIAS_GROUPS contains 5 group entries (create:agent, create:testing-playbook, memory:save, deep-research, deep-review); no cli-opencode group nor token in any alias array.
evidence: Strategy doc says 101 touched aliases.ts to add cli-opencode advisor alias. Live file has no cli-opencode token. Either narrative drift or aliases.ts edit was lost.
recommendation: Either add cli-opencode group to aliases.ts if executor-routing alias was intended OR update 101+102 narrative to clarify the alias actually lives in cli-opencode SKILL.md trigger phrases.
